response.justification: Candidate has 15+ years of broad full-stack experience with recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, which aligns with the AI-native engineering mindset the role requires. However, the resume shows no strong Python signal (skills list omits Python entirely) and limited evidence of large-scale distributed systems ownership at Staff level — the Sociomantic role hints at high-throughput work but in DLang, not Python. Title was Staff at re:cinq but tenure was short (6 months) and current career gap plus the heavy small-shop/founder history raises questions about operating at the scale and pace this role demands.
strengths: Recent hands-on work with MCP/agentic developer tooling matches the AI-native baseline
High-throughput backend exposure at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of req/s)
Long track record of TDD, CI/CD, testing craft and knowledge sharing
concerns: Python is not listed in skills despite being a must-have
No clear evidence of distributed-systems architectural leadership or on-call ownership at scale
Short Staff tenure (6 months) followed by a current career gap
